scope_note: QA Koenig Spread Optimality cert [292]. Primary sources: Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions Wild Egg Books ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (spread s=sin^2 of angle between lines, quadrance, rational trig); Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers Oxford ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5 Ch.III (Farey sequences, Ford circles, Diophantine approximation). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): Define G_tilde(b,e)=b^2+e^2 (blue quadrance of direction vector) and s(b,e)=e^2/G_tilde (spread of direction (b,e) from horizontal). Then: (1) SPREAD_ID: I(b,e)=|b^2-2e^2| = 3*G_tilde*|s-1/3| for all b,e>=1 (exact algebraic identity; eliminates sqrt(2)); (2) NO_EXACT: no (b,e) in Z>0 x Z>0 satisfies s=1/3 exactly (equivalent to sqrt(2) irrational; the spread-1/3 direction has no rational representative); (3) PELL_OPT: I(b,e)=1 iff |s-1/3|=1/(3*G_tilde) -- Pell solutions achieve the minimum nonzero spread deviation at their scale; (4) INTER_SPREAD: for consecutive Pell pairs (b_n,e_n),(b_{n+1},e_{n+1}), the Wildberger spread between the two direction lines = 1/(G_tilde_n*G_tilde_{n+1}), monotonically decreasing; (5) ALT_SIDE: consecutive Pell spreads lie on alternating sides of 1/3. The sqrt(2) cusp is characterized purely as 'the direction with spread 1/3' -- no irrational numbers in statement or proof. Theorem NT: all computations integer or Fraction; no float state. Mechanism chain: cert [289] (Koenig Pell Ford Circle); cert [141] (QA Pell Norm). Lineage: derived 2026-06-01.
